questions.question: Which of the following is a supervised learning algorithm?
What is the primary purpose of an activation function in a neural network?
options: To reduce the number of parameters
To introduce non-linearity into the model
To speed up the training process

questions.question: What does the 'p-value' represent in statistical hypothesis testing?
options: The probability that the null hypothesis is true
The probability of observing the data given that the null hypothesis is true
The probability of making a Type II error
The strength of the alternative hypothesis

questions.question: Which architecture is primarily used in state-of-the-art Large Language Models (LLMs) like GPT-4?
In the context of Big Data, what does the term 'ETL' stand for?
What is 'Overfitting' in a machine learning model?
options: When the model performs well on training data but poorly on unseen data
When the model is too simple to capture the underlying patterns
When the model has too few parameters
When the training data is too small

questions.question: Which optimizer is known for using adaptive learning rates for each parameter?
What is the main operation used in Convolutional Neural Networks to extract spatial features?
What is the purpose of a 'Confusion Matrix'?
options: To visualize the correlation between features
To evaluate the performance of a classification model
To handle missing values in a dataset

questions.question: What is 'Algorithmic Bias'?
options: A mathematical error in the code
Systematic and unfair discrimination in the outputs of an AI system
The preference for one programming language over another
The speed difference between two algorithms
